title: Joe Kent, a Top Counterterrorism Official for the Trump Administration, Resigns, Citing Iran War
summary: Mr. Kent is the highest-ranking Trump administration official to quit in opposition to the conflict. He said pressure from Israel had pushed the president into war against Iran.

title: Israel’s Killing of Ali Larijani Could Allow Military to Tighten Grip on Iran
summary: As Iran’s top national security official, Mr. Larijani had a reputation for acting as a bridge between hard-line figures in the armed forces and more moderate political factions.

title: Dozens Dead in Pakistani Airstrike on Kabul
summary: The attack hit a drug rehabilitation facility, and Afghanistan vowed to retaliate, risking an escalation of the conflict between the two countries.

title: Why Are Pakistan and Afghanistan Fighting?
summary: The renewed violence between the neighboring countries stems from Pakistan’s accusations that Afghanistan’s Taliban government has harbored a militant group.

title: What’s in Trump’s SAVE Act, the Voter ID Bill Republicans Are Pushing?
summary: The legislation would require voters to prove their citizenship in person upon registration, ban IDs without a photo at polling places and criminalize failures to enforce such requirements.

title: Democrats Hammer Trump on ‘Energy Affordability’
summary: A new Senate report argues the Trump administration has also pushed costs up by stifling clean energy.

title: War in Iran Shows Growing Conservative Divide Over Israel
summary: As the U.S.-Israel-Iran war continues, conservatism’s most famous figures  are in a rhetorical brawl over America’s role.

title: Trump’s Gutting of Election Security Fuels Worries for Midterms
summary: Officials say the crippling of the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency, which the president himself created, could open elections to cyberattacks and foreign influence.

title: A St. Patrick’s Day Election in Chicago? Politicians Look for Luck in the Timing.
summary: In a county with the largest Irish American population in the U.S., a crush of parades has been a bounty for candidates searching for votes.

title: China Is Embracing OpenClaw, a New A.I. Agent, and the Government Is Wary
summary: Excitement about A.I. assistant tools is running into growing concerns about the security risks of software that operates autonomously on user’s devices.

title: John Lithgow’s Career Spans 200 Roles — From ‘3rd Rock’ to Roald Dahl
summary: Is there anyone John Lithgow can’t — or won’t — play?

title: Inside the Supply Line Delivering American Guns to Mexican Cartels
summary: A surge of weapons is flowing from the U.S. to Mexico. These firearms — sourced from gun shops, shows, websites and apps — are funneled across the border to fuel the country’s most violent crimes.

title: Trump Signals He May Intervene in Cuba Following Iran and Venezuela
summary: Still at war with Iran and in control in Venezuela, President Trump is signaling that he is about to intervene in another country.

title: Peter Thiel Fears the Antichrist Is Coming. In Rome, Some Call His View Heresy.
summary: The right-wing tech investor is giving lectures near the Catholic church’s administrative heart. Commentators there are rejecting his apocalyptic vision.

title: Judge Ejects Federal Prosecutor From Court and Orders Bosses to Testify
summary: Judge Zahid Quraishi ordered a hearing on who had the authority to lead New Jersey’s top federal law enforcement office.

title: How Readers Voted on Miscast Roles in the Movies
summary: Given a list of 14 mismatches, they quickly picked Ben Platt in “Dear Evan Hansen.” But they took issue (loudly) with one of our choices.

title: In the Oscars Audience, Stars Caught Their Breath
summary: Our photographer captured unguarded moments with Michael B. Jordan, Paul Thomas Anderson, Gwyneth Paltrow and other celebrities in the crowd.

title: Academy ‘Extremely Upset’ After Teyana Taylor Says Oscars Security Shoved Her
summary: The academy said an employee of an outside security firm hired for the Oscars had “incidental contact” with the actress, which it called “not acceptable.”

title: Mix-and-Match These Stunning Dishes to Gather Loved Ones and Feast
summary: Make one or make all: Yewande Komolafe’s curated menus are meant for celebrating.

title: Trump’s Dangerous Lack of a Strategy in Iran
summary: The early reality of the Iran war is not cooperating with the president’s bluster.

title: The Scrappy Mayor Showing Democrats How It’s Done
summary: Democrats can’t just sit back and expect the prevailing political winds to produce a blue wave.

title: Mamdani Moves to End City’s Defense of Adams in Sexual Assault Lawsuit
summary: New York City has been representing former Mayor Eric Adams in a civil lawsuit concerning an alleged sexual assault in the 1990s.

title: When Teens Are Pressured to Send Sexual Images
summary: Adolescents said they were more likely to share photos with a dating partner, or when they had been worn down with repeated requests.

title: Christopher A. Sims, 83, Dies; Won Nobel for Modeling Economic Policy
summary: He and Thomas J. Sargent shared the prize in 2011 for devising statistical tools to help guide economic policymakers.

title: A Meteor Streaks Across the U.S. and Rattles Ohio With an Explosive Boom
summary: NASA confirmed that the loud boom heard and felt by people in northern Ohio was indeed caused by a meteor.
